title: erigeron mayoensis
intro: Erigeron mayoensis, also known as Mayo's fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations, and may also have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Erigeron mayoensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for promoting overall health and well-being.
